title: Papadimitriou–Yannakakis NP-completeness Conjecture
statement_text: The Exact Matching problem (EM) is NP-complete.
context_text: Papadimitriou and Yannakakis introduced EM in 1982 while studying restricted minimum weight spanning tree problems, and conjectured it to be NP-complete. This was later cast into doubt when Mulmuley, Vazirani and Vazirani showed EM $\in$ RP, making NP-hardness unlikely under standard complexity assumptions.
paper_contribution: Proves EM is solvable in deterministic polynomial time for graphs of independence number at most $\alpha$ (running time $n^{O(2^{O(\alpha)})}$) and for bipartite graphs of bipartite independence number at most $\beta$, providing new infinite families of graph classes for which EM is in P.
paper_title: Exact Matching in Graphs of Bounded Independence Number

title: Conjecture 3
statement_text: The problem of counting perfect matchings in input graphs of independence number $2$ is $\#P$-complete.
context_text: The authors note that for all previously known graph classes on which EM is tractable (planar graphs, $K_{3,3}$-minor-free graphs, complete and complete bipartite graphs), counting perfect matchings is also polynomial. Their new results for graphs of independence number 2 break this alignment, since no polynomial counting scheme is known for this class and the authors believe counting is hard.
notes: The paper text is truncated before Section 6 (open problems), so additional open problems stated there could not be extracted.
paper_title: Exact Matching in Graphs of Bounded Independence Number
